content:
# 🐇 Cheat Sheet: Building Apps with AI for Bunnies

Keep this open in another tab. Everything you'll look up at 2am.

---

## API Quick Reference

### OpenAI — JavaScript

```js
// Basic completion
const res = await openai.chat.completions.create({
  model: "gpt-4o",
  messages: [
    { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
    { role: "user", content: userMessage }
  ],
  max_tokens: 500,
  temperature: 0.7
});
const text = res.choices[0].message.content;

// Streaming
const stream = await openai.chat.completions.create({
  model: "gpt-4o",
  messages: [...],
  stream: true
});
for await (const chunk of stream) {
  process.stdout.write(chunk.choices[0]?.delta?.content ?? "");
}

// JSON mode
const res = await openai.chat.completions.create({
  model: "gpt-4o",
  response_format: { type: "json_object" },
  messages: [
    { role: "system", content: "Respond only with valid JSON." },
    { role: "user", content: prompt }
  ]
});
const data = JSON.parse(res.choices[0].message.content);

// Embeddings
const res = await openai.embeddings.create({
  model: "text-embedding-3-small",
  input: "text to embed"
});
const vector = res.data[0].embedding;
```

### OpenAI — Python

```python
# Basic completion
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
        {"role": "user", "content": user_message}
    ],
    max_tokens=500,
    temperature=0.7
)
text = response.choices[0].message.content

# Streaming
stream = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o", messages=[...], stream=True
)
for chunk in stream:
    print(chunk.choices[0].delta.content or "", end="")

# JSON mode
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_format={"type": "json_object"},
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Respond only with valid JSON."},
        {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
    ]
)
data = json.loads(response.choices[0].message.content)

# Embeddings
response = client.embeddings.create(
    model="text-embedding-3-small",
    input="text to embed"
)
vector = response.data[0].embedding
```

---

## The 6-Component Prompt Anatomy

Every strong prompt has some combination of:

| # | Component | Example |
|---|-----------|---------|
| 1 | **Role** | "You are a quiz question generator." |
| 2 | **Task** | "Generate 5 multiple-choice questions." |
| 3 | **Context** | "The student is in 9th grade." |
| 4 | **Format** | "Return a JSON array of objects with keys: question, options, answer." |
| 5 | **Constraints** | "Each question must be unique. No trick questions." |
| 6 | **Examples** | "Example output: [{\"question\":\"...\",\"options\":[...],\"answer\":\"B\"}]" |

Not every prompt needs all 6. But if output is wrong, add whichever component is missing.

---

## Prompt Template Patterns

### System Prompt Skeleton
```
You are a [role] that helps [target user] with [task domain].

When given [input type], you will [desired behavior].

Always [constraint 1].
Never [constraint 2].
Respond in [language/tone/format].
```

### JSON Output Request
```
You are a data extractor. Given user input, return ONLY valid JSON.
No explanation. No markdown. No code fences. Just the raw JSON object.

Schema:
{
  "field1": "string",
  "field2": "number",
  "field3": ["array", "of", "strings"]
}
```

### Chain-of-Thought (for reasoning tasks)
```
Think through this step by step before answering.

1. First, identify [X].
2. Then, consider [Y].
3. Finally, produce [Z].

Show your reasoning, then provide your final answer after "ANSWER:".
```

### Few-Shot Example Pattern
```
Here are examples of the transformation I want:

Input: "The sky is blue."
Output: {"subject": "sky", "attribute": "blue", "confidence": "high"}

Input: "She might be tired."
Output: {"subject": "she", "attribute": "tired", "confidence": "low"}

Now apply this to: "[USER_INPUT]"
```

### Fallback / Uncertainty Handling
```
If you don't know the answer or the input is unclear, respond with:
{"error": "insufficient_information", "reason": "brief explanation"}

Never guess. Never make up facts. Only respond confidently when you're sure.
```

---

## Environment Variables Checklist

Copy this into your `.env` and fill in every line before deploying.

```
# AI Provider
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# App Config
NODE_ENV=production
PORT=3000

# Database (if using)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/dbname

# Frontend (Vite — must prefix with VITE_)
VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://your-api.railway.app

# Auth (if using)
JWT_SECRET=long-random-string-minimum-32-chars
SESSION_SECRET=another-long-random-string

# Rate Limiting
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS=60000
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS=20

# Logging
LOG_LEVEL=info
```

**Never commit `.env` to Git.**
Add `.env` and `.env.local` to `.gitignore` before your first commit.

---

## Deployment Commands

### Vercel (Frontend)

```bash
# Install CLI
npm i -g vercel

# Login
vercel login

# Deploy (preview)
vercel

# Deploy (production)
vercel --prod

# Set environment variable
vercel env add VITE_API_BASE_URL production

# Pull env vars to local
vercel env pull .env.local

# View deployment logs
vercel logs your-app-name
```

### Railway (Backend)

```bash
# Install CLI
npm i -g @railway/cli

# Login
railway login

# Link to project
railway link

# Deploy current directory
railway up

# Set environment variable
railway variables set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# View logs
railway logs

# Open project dashboard
railway open

# Run command in Railway environment locally
railway run node index.js
```

### Docker (self-hosted)

```bash
# Build image
docker build -t my-ai-app .

# Run locally
docker run -p 3000:3000 --env-file .env my-ai-app

# Push to registry
docker tag my-ai-app registry.example.com/my-ai-app:latest
docker push registry.example.com/my-ai-app:latest
```

---

## Cost Estimation Formula

```
cost = (input_tokens / 1,000,000) × input_price
     + (output_tokens / 1,000,000) × output_price
```

### Current Pricing (verify at platform.openai.com/pricing)

| Model | Input per 1M | Output per 1M |
|-------|-------------|---------------|
| gpt-4o | $2.50 | $10.00 |
| gpt-4o-mini | $0.15 | $0.60 |
| text-embedding-3-small | $0.02 | — |
| text-embedding-3-large | $0.13 | — |

### Monthly Cost Estimate Template

```
Users per day:          ___
Requests per user:      ___
Avg input tokens:       ___
Avg output tokens:      ___

Daily requests = users × requests_per_user
Daily tokens   = daily_requests × (input + output)
Monthly tokens = daily_tokens × 30

Monthly cost = (monthly_input_tokens / 1M × input_price)
             + (monthly_output_tokens / 1M × output_price)
```

### Token Rules of Thumb

- 1 token ≈ 4 characters in English
- 1 token ≈ ¾ of a word
- 750 words ≈ 1,000 tokens
- 1 page of text ≈ 500–700 tokens
- A typical API request (system + user + response) ≈ 300–800 tokens
- GPT-4o context window: 128,000 tokens max

---

## HTTP Status Codes for AI Apps

| Code | Meaning | What to do |
|------|---------|------------|
| 200 | OK | All good |
| 400 | Bad Request | Check your request body / missing fields |
| 401 | Unauthorized | API key missing or invalid |
| 403 | Forbidden | Key exists but lacks permission |
| 404 | Not Found | Wrong endpoint URL |
| 422 | Unprocessable Entity | Request valid but semantically wrong |
| 429 | Too Many Requests | Rate limited — back off and retry |
| 500 | Internal Server Error | Their problem (or yours — check logs) |
| 502 | Bad Gateway | Upstream AI service is unreachable |
| 503 | Service Unavailable | AI provider is down — check status page |

**OpenAI Status Page:** status.openai.com

---

## Rate Limit Handling

### Exponential Backoff (JavaScript)

```js
async function callWithRetry(fn, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (err) {
      if (err.status === 429 && attempt < maxRetries) {
        const delay = Math.pow(2, attempt) * 1000 + Math.random() * 500;
        console.log(`Rate limited. Retrying in ${delay}ms...`);
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delay));
      } else {
        throw err;
      }
    }
  }
}

// Usage
const result = await callWithRetry(() =>
  openai.chat.completions.create({ model: "gpt-4o", messages })
);
```

### Rate Limit Headers to Watch

```
x-ratelimit-limit-requests       — max requests per minute
x-ratelimit-remaining-requests   — requests left in window
x-ratelimit-reset-requests       — when window resets (ISO 8601)
x-ratelimit-limit-tokens         — max tokens per minute
x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens     — tokens left in window
```

---

## Common Errors → Fixes

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `401 Incorrect API key` | Wrong or missing key | Check `OPENAI_API_KEY` in env |
| `429 Rate limit exceeded` | Too many requests | Add retry with backoff |
| `400 max_tokens too large` | Exceeds model limit | Reduce `max_tokens` or trim input |
| `context_length_exceeded` | Prompt too long | Truncate input, summarize history |
| `JSON.parse error` | Model returned non-JSON | Use JSON mode + validate output |
| `CORS error` | Frontend calling AI API directly | Route through your backend |
| `Cannot read properties of undefined` | Empty API response | Check `choices[0]?.message?.content` |
| `ECONNREFUSED` | Backend not running | Start your server |
| `fetch is not defined` | Node < 18 | Upgrade Node or `npm i node-fetch` |
| `Billing hard limit reached` | Spent your budget cap | Raise limit at platform.openai.com |

---

## Express Backend Boilerplate

```js
import express from "express";
import cors from "cors";
import OpenAI from "openai";

const app = express();
const openai = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY });

app.use(cors({ origin: process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGIN || "*" }));
app.use(express.json());

app.post("/api/generate", async (req, res) => {
  const { prompt } = req.body;
  if (!prompt) return res.status(400).json({ error: "prompt required" });

  try {
    const completion = await openai.chat.completions.create({
      model: "gpt-4o-mini",
      messages: [
        { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
        { role: "user", content: prompt }
      ],
      max_tokens: 500
    });
    res.json({ result: completion.choices[0].message.content });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err);
    res.status(err.status || 500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.listen(process.env.PORT || 3000, () =>
  console.log(`Running on port ${process.env.PORT || 3000}`)
);
```

---

## React Hook: useAI

```js
import { useState } from "react";

export function useAI() {
  const [result, setResult] = useState("");
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);

  async function generate(prompt) {
    setLoading(true);
    setError(null);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`${import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL}/api/generate`, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ prompt })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
      const data = await res.json();
      setResult(data.result);
    } catch (err) {
      setError(err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }

  return { result, loading, error, generate };
}
```

---

## Git Checklist Before First Commit

```bash
# 1. Initialize
git init
git branch -M main

# 2. Create .gitignore
echo "node_modules/\n.env\n.env.local\ndist/\n.DS_Store" >> .gitignore

# 3. Verify .env is ignored
git status  # .env should NOT appear

# 4. First commit
git add .
git commit -m "initial commit"

# 5. Push to GitHub
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/your-repo.git
git push -u origin main
```

---

## The One-Page Mental Model

```
User Input
    ↓
[Frontend] — validate, show loading state
    ↓ HTTP POST
[Your Backend] — sanitize, add system prompt, call AI API
    ↓ OpenAI SDK
[AI Model] — generates response
    ↓ JSON response
[Your Backend] — parse, validate, log, return to frontend
    ↓ HTTP 200
[Frontend] — display result, handle errors
```

**Your backend is the gatekeeper.** Never call AI APIs from the frontend. Never expose your API key. Never trust AI output without validation.

---

*"First make it work. Then make it fast. Then make it cheap."*

*— Every AI engineer, eventually*
